# Agentified

> Context intelligence layer for AI agents. Registers all your tools, then assembles the right context — tools, messages, and memory — for each agent turn via hybrid ranking. Framework-agnostic (Mastra, LangGraph, LangChain, raw SDKs). 86% token cost reduction with same task accuracy.

Agentified is not another agent framework. It's a context layer that sits between your agent and its tools, selecting only what's relevant per turn instead of dumping everything into the prompt.

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│               Your Agent                     │
│     (Mastra / LangGraph / any framework)     │
└──────────────────┬──────────────────────────┘
                   │
      session.context.assemble()
                   │
┌──────────────────▼──────────────────────────┐
│            Agentified SDK                    │
│          (TypeScript / Python)               │
│                                              │
│  .tools(...)  .messages(...)  .assemble()    │
│         → AssembledContext                    │
│      { tools, messages, tokenEstimate }      │
└──────────────────┬──────────────────────────┘
                   │
          register + discover
                   │
┌──────────────────▼──────────────────────────┐
│           agentified-core                    │
│            (Rust server)                     │
│                                              │
│  Hybrid ranking · Session continuity ·       │
│  Graph expansion · Embedding cache           │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Three steps: (1) Register tools — agentified-core embeds and indexes them. (2) Assemble context — `session.context.messages(...).assemble()` returns ranked tools + messages. (3) Execute — pass assembled context to your agent framework.

## Quick Start

TypeScript:

```bash
pnpm add agentified
```

```typescript
import { Agentified } from "agentified";

const ag = new Agentified();
await ag.connect("http://localhost:9119");

const dataset = await ag.dataset("my-agent").register({
  tools: [
    { name: "get_weather", description: "Get current weather",
      parameters: { type: "object", properties: { city: { type: "string" } }, required: ["city"] },
      handler: async (args) => ({ temp: 22 }) },
  ],
});

const session = dataset.session("chat-1");
const ctx = await session.context.messages({ strategy: "recent" }).assemble();
// ctx.tools     → ranked tools object
// ctx.messages  → conversation history
// ctx.tokenEstimate → estimated token count
```

Python:

```bash
pip install agentified
```

```python
from agentified import Agentified, BackendTool, RegisterInput

ag = Agentified()
await ag.connect("http://localhost:9119")

instance = await ag.register(RegisterInput(tools=[
    BackendTool(name="get_weather", description="Get current weather",
                parameters={"type": "object", "properties": {"city": {"type": "string"}}},
                handler=lambda args: {"temp": 22, "city": args["city"]}),
]))

session = instance.session("chat-1")
ctx = await session.context.messages(strategy="recent").assemble()
```

Run the server:

```bash
docker run -p 9119:9119 -e OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-... agentified/agentified-core
```

## Core Concepts

### Context Assembly

The fluent API `session.context.tools(...).messages(...).assemble()` assembles an `AssembledContext` containing ranked tools, conversation history, and a token estimate. One call per agent turn replaces manual context wiring.

### Hybrid Ranking

Tool discovery uses a hybrid algorithm: `final_score = 0.7 × semantic + 0.3 × bm25_normalized`.

- Semantic (70%): cosine similarity via OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` across 4 weighted fields — description (0.5), input_schema (0.3), name (0.1), output_schema (0.1).
- BM25 (30%): keyword matching on concatenated tool text. Catches exact matches the embedding model might miss.

Weights are customizable per discover request via `embedding_weights`.

### Session Continuity

Turns are captured via `capture_turn()` tracking which tools were used. On subsequent discovers, previously-used tools from the same session are prioritized (injected with score=1.0), preventing context breakage mid-conversation.

### Graph Expansion

Tools declare `metadata.requires` / `metadata.provides` relationships. After ranking, dependency tools are auto-injected (up to 60% of the tool limit).

### Tool Discovery

`session.discoverTool` is a callable tool that agents can invoke at runtime to find additional tools beyond the initially assembled set. Useful for open-ended workflows.

## REST API

agentified-core (Rust/Axum) endpoints:

| Method | Path | Purpose |
|--------|------|---------|
| GET | /health | Health check |
| POST | /api/v1/datasets/{id}/tools | Register tools (batch embed + store) |
| GET | /api/v1/datasets/{id}/tools | List registered tools |
| POST | /api/v1/datasets/{id}/discover | Discover relevant tools (hybrid ranking) |
| POST | /api/v1/turns | Capture a turn for session continuity |
| POST | /api/v1/messages | Append messages to a conversation |
| GET | /api/v1/messages | Retrieve conversation messages |
| POST | /api/v1/context | Get context with strategy (recent / full) |

Configuration:

| Variable | Required | Default | Description |
|----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| OPENAI_API_KEY | Yes | — | OpenAI API key for text-embedding-3-small |
| AGENTIFIED_PORT | No | 9119 | HTTP server port |
| AGENTIFIED_STORAGE | No | — | Set to "sqlite" for persistence |
| AGENTIFIED_DB_PATH | No | ./agentified.db | SQLite database file path |

Storage: in-memory by default (fast, no persistence), SQLite WAL mode for persistence (async write-through, concurrent reads).

## TypeScript SDK

Package: `agentified` (npm)

Key classes: `Agentified` → `DatasetRef` → `Instance` → `Session` → `ContextBuilder` → `AssembledContext`.

- `Agentified`: entry point. `connect(url)` or auto-spawn local server.
- `DatasetRef`: namespace for tool sets. `dataset("name").register({ tools })`.
- `Instance`: holds registered tools. `instance.session("id")` creates sessions.
- `Session`: multi-turn context. `session.context` returns a `ContextBuilder`.
- `ContextBuilder`: fluent API — `.tools(limit)`, `.messages({ strategy, maxTokens })`, `.assemble()`.
- `AssembledContext`: `{ tools, messages, tokenEstimate }`.

Tool types: `BackendTool` (server-side handler), `ClientTool` (browser-side, `type: "client"`), `McpTool` (MCP server, `type: "mcp"`).

Additional features: `session.discoverTool` (runtime tool discovery), `session.updateConversation()` (persist messages with dedup), `session.prepareStep()` (auto-capture turns), event callbacks via `onEvent`.

## Python SDK

Package: `agentified` (PyPI)

Mirrors the TypeScript SDK. Async-first with `SyncAgentified` wrapper available.

Key classes: `Agentified` (async context manager) → `Instance` → `Session` → `ContextBuilder` → `AssembledContext`.

```python
async with Agentified() as ag:
    await ag.connect("http://localhost:9119")
    instance = await ag.register(RegisterInput(tools=[...]))
    session = instance.session("chat-1")
    ctx = await session.context.messages(strategy="recent").assemble()
```

## Integrations

### Mastra (TypeScript)

Package: `agentified-mastra`. Adapter wraps SDK for Mastra agents. `AssembledContext.tools` are Mastra `StructuredTool` objects. Supports `prepareStep` and `discoverTool` hooks.

### LangChain (Python)

Package: `agentified-langchain`. Adapter wraps SDK for LangChain/LangGraph. `AssembledContext.tools` are LangChain `StructuredTool` objects.

### React / Frontend

Packages: `agentified-react`, `agentified-fe-client`. Provides `AgentifiedProvider`, `useAgentified()` (chat hook), `useAgentifiedTool()` (register frontend tool handlers), and `Inspector` (debug panel showing tool calls, timing, tokens). Frontend tools execute client-side with automatic result injection.

## Examples

| Example | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| ts-sdk-smoke | TypeScript SDK basics — register, discover, sessions |
| ts-mastra-smoke | Mastra + OpenAI agent with AG-UI streaming |
| py-sdk-smoke | Python SDK basics — register, discover, sessions |
| py-langchain-sdk-smoke | LangChain + OpenAI agent |
| quickhr | Full-stack: Mastra backend + React frontend + HR domain |
| py-langgraph | LangGraph + Gemini (Python) |

## Optional

### Benchmarks

| Metric | Baseline | Agentified | Improvement |
|--------|----------|------------|-------------|
| Task Correctness | 0.98 | 0.98 | — |
| Avg Tokens/Request | 45,000 | 6,200 | -86% |
| Cost per 1K queries | $21.53 | $2.95 | -86% |
| Latency (p50) | 2.1s | 1.4s | -33% |

### Project Structure

```
agentified/
├── src/core/              # Rust Axum server (SUL licensed)
├── src/ts-packages/sdk/   # TypeScript SDK (MIT)
├── src/ts-packages/mastra/ # Mastra adapter (MIT)
├── src/ts-packages/react/ # React provider + hooks (MIT)
├── src/ts-packages/fe-client/ # Frontend client (MIT)
├── src/py-packages/sdk/   # Python SDK (MIT)
├── src/py-packages/langchain/ # LangChain adapter (MIT)
├── examples/              # Runnable examples
├── benchmarks/            # Performance benchmarks
└── docs/                  # Documentation
```
